title: Palicourea pavetta
taxonomy: Palicourea pavetta belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Rubiaceae is a large and diverse family of flowering plants, consisting of over 13,000 species. Palicourea pavetta is a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical Africa. The species is characterized by its woody habit and produces small, white flowers.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
